notes: Confirmed: server-side Blazor rendering is not supported in SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor. SKCanvasView has [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] and JSModuleInterop uses JSHost.ImportAsync which throws PlatformNotSupportedException on non-WASM runtimes. Creating a Blazor Server app with SKCanvasView and @rendermode InteractiveServer builds successfully but throws PlatformNotSupportedException at runtime. The feature requested (marshaling SkiaSharp draw calls over SignalR for server-side Blazor) does not exist yet. No server-side or SignalR references exist anywhere in the Blazor views package.

reproductionSteps.description: Inspect SKCanvasView source for platform restrictions — confirms [SupportedOSPlatform("browser")] annotation
Inspect JSModuleInterop for browser-only requirement — uses JSHost.ImportAsync (WASM-only) and NotSupportedException fallback
Verify csproj limits SupportedPlatform to browser only and depends on NativeAssets.WebAssembly
Create Blazor Server app with SKCanvasView using InteractiveServer render mode
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazor -n Repro --framework net10.0 --interactivity Server && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.0-stable.1
reproductionSteps.output:
Restore succeeded.
PackageReference for package 'SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor' version '3.119.0-stable.1' added.

filesCreated.description: Blazor page using SKCanvasView with @rendermode InteractiveServer to test server-side rendering

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor Server app — build succeeds with no compile-time block for server-side usage
Run Blazor Server app and navigate with browser — SKCanvasView throws PlatformNotSupportedException, confirming server-side rendering is not supported
reproductionSteps.output: Server started. Browser navigated to app. Page renders heading but SKCanvasView component is absent. Browser console error: System.PlatformNotSupportedException: System.Runtime.InteropServices.JavaScript is not supported on this platform. at JSHost.ImportAsync() at JSModuleInterop..ctor() at SKHtmlCanvasInterop.ImportAsync() at SKCanvasView.OnAfterRenderAsync(). Circuit terminated with unhandled exception.

versionResults.notes: Feature request — server-side Blazor rendering does not exist. SKCanvasView throws PlatformNotSupportedException when used with InteractiveServer render mode, confirming the feature is missing.
Multi-version testing skipped — this is a feature request. The source code confirms no server-side Blazor rendering path exists on any version.

actionability.reason: Client-side WASM support was delivered via SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor (PR #1811), but the core feature request — server-side Blazor rendering via SignalR marshaling — remains unimplemented. High community interest (19 reactions, 48 comments).

proposedResponse.body: Thanks for this feature request and the ongoing discussion. We investigated the current state of server-side Blazor support. The `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` package is designed exclusively for client-side WASM execution: - `SKCanvasView` has `[SupportedOSPlatform("browser")]` - `JSModuleInterop` uses `JSHost.ImportAsync` which throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` on non-WASM runtimes - The `.csproj` limits `SupportedPlatform` to `browser` only When used with `@rendermode InteractiveServer`, `SKCanvasView` throws `PlatformNotSupportedException` at runtime — the circuit terminates with an unhandled exception. The client-side Blazor WASM support was shipped in `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` (merged in #1811). The remaining gap is server-side Blazor support, which would require a fundamentally different architecture (e.g., serializing draw commands over SignalR as explored in the [BlazorSkiaSharp prototype](https://github.com/mattleibow/BlazorSkiaSharp)). For server-side scenarios today: 1. **Static image rendering** — Use SkiaSharp on the server to render to PNG/JPEG and serve via `` tags 2. **Interactive WASM render mode (.NET 8+)** — Keep business logic on the server while `SKCanvasView` renders client-side in WASM

workarounds: Use SkiaSharp on the server to render static images (PNG/JPEG) and serve via tags
Use Blazor Interactive WebAssembly render mode (.NET 8+) to run SKCanvasView client-side while keeping business logic on the server
